title: CS2 Skin Price Tracker & Market Overview | CSGOLuck Wiki
description: Real-time CS2 skin price tracking and market analysis. Compare prices across all skins, find the cheapest deals, track market trends, and analyze price distributions by rarity and category.
heading: CS2 Skin Price Tracker
intro: Explore live pricing data for all CS2 weapon skins, knives, and gloves. Track market trends, find the best deals, and analyze price distributions by rarity and weapon category.

faqs.question: What factors affect CS2 skin prices?
faqs.answer: CS2 skin prices are influenced by rarity tier, wear condition (float), whether the skin is StatTrak or Souvenir, case discontinuation, pro player popularity, in-game meta shifts, and overall market supply and demand.

faqs.question: Why do some cheap skins cost less than a dollar?
faqs.answer: Consumer Grade and Industrial Grade skins have lower rarity and are more common drops from cases or trades. These entry-level skins are perfect for beginners or players building a collection on a budget.

faqs.question: Do knife prices follow the same trends as weapon skins?
faqs.answer: Knives are rarer and typically much more expensive than weapon skins. While they follow general market trends, knife prices are more volatile because supply is extremely limited and demand is high among collectors.

faqs.question: What is a Factory New skin worth compared to Battle-Scarred?
faqs.answer: Factory New (FN) skins are typically 2–10x more expensive than Battle-Scarred versions of the same skin, depending on rarity and demand. The price difference reflects both aesthetics and collector preference.

faqs.question: How often do skin prices update?
faqs.answer: Prices on CSGOLuck Wiki are sourced from multiple marketplaces and updated regularly. The exact update frequency varies by marketplace, but prices typically reflect current market conditions within hours.

faqs.question: Should I invest in expensive skins or cheap skins?
faqs.answer: Investment potential varies. Cheap skins can appreciate quickly if a case is discontinued or a pro uses the skin in-game. Expensive skins (Covert, knives) tend to hold value better but require more capital. Diversification is key.
